mnemonics: CLT ZIPS: Central Limit Theorem Z-tests for Independent Population Samples (reminds you the core condition for comparing large samples is independence and that you use a Z-test)
keyPoints: For large samples (typically n > 30), the sampling distribution of the difference between means is approximately normal by the Central Limit Theorem.
The appropriate test statistic is the Z-score, calculated using the standard error of the difference between two independent means.
The procedure assumes the samples are independent of each other, and the variances are either known or the sample sizes are large enough that the sample variances are good approximations.
commonMistakes: Using a t-test instead of a Z-test for large, independent samples. While a t-test is more conservative, the Z-test is the standard and theoretically correct approach when the conditions of the CLT are met.
Failing to verify that the samples are truly independent. For example, using this test on paired or matched data (e.g., 'before and after' measurements) is incorrect and requires a different method.
